claims.claim: Using llama.cpp with Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct (Q4_K_M) across six synthetic-but-messy scenarios with two repeats each, we compare an unconstrained transcript-wrapper persistence mode (commit every proposal) against a typed quarantine mode that classifies proposals by risk and routes moderate-risk items to delayed critic review while rejecting high-risk items.

evidence_refs.quote: We evaluate whether a typed memory quarantine queue reduces poisoned and stale long-term memory retention when memory proposals are generated by a real local LLM processing messy multi-turn workflow transcripts, extending prior work that relied on deterministic or stochastic toy proposal generators.
